records.stage_evidence: Discharge Summary with dates of admission, procedure, and discharge; advice at discharge includes medications and follow-up

ocr.verbatim:
HEART CARE CENTRE
Discharge Summary
Name of patient:
Address:
Consultant:
Age/sex: 51Y/M
UHID 109983
WARD HDU
DATE OF ADMISSION: 02/04/2026
DATE OF PROCEDURE: 03/04/2026
DATE OF DISCHARGE: 04/04/2026
Diagnosis:
1- CAD, Acute AWM I
2- CAG-SVD
3- PTCA/s to LAD ( 2 Stent)
4- LVEF-40%
Investigations: All report attached.
Procedure done : Coronary angiogram with PTCA/s to LAD
Stent Name : DES-2.5 X 12mm.
DES-3.0 X 20mm.
Result: Improvement.
Brief summary: Patient was admitted with chest pain & AWM I. He underwent coronary angiogram which revealed single vessel disease. He was advised for PTCA/s to LAD. PTCA/s to LAD was done & flow was established. He was discharge in stable condition.
Advice at discharge:
1. T- Brilinta 90mg 1tab BD (9:00am- 9:00pm)
2. T- Ecosprin 75 mg 1tab OD ( 9:00pm)
3. T-Rozat 20mg 1tab OD
4. C-Pantocid-DSR 1cap BBF
5. T- Levoflox 250 mg 1tab OD 3 days then stop
6. T- Calpol 650 mg 1 tab SOS
*Brilinta & Ecosprin should not be stopped without advice.
Next Review : To see Dr. Deep Pant after 7 days in OPD Room No-1
Emergency contact no -

records.summary: This is a discharge summary from a Heart Care Centre for a 51-year-old male patient who underwent a coronary angiogram and PTCA/s to the left anterior descending artery (LAD) with two stents. The patient was admitted for acute coronary syndrome (AWMI) and discharged in stable condition on 04/04/2026. Medications and follow-up instructions are provided.
key_observations: Diagnosis: CAD, Acute AWM I, CAG-SVD, LVEF-40%
Procedure: Coronary angiogram with PTCA/s to LAD (2 stents)
Medications prescribed at discharge including Brilinta, Ecosprin, Rozat, Pantocid-DSR, Levoflox, and Calpol
Follow-up: See Dr. Deep Pant after 7 days in OPD Room No-1

CORONARY ANGIOGRAPHY REPORT
Pt. Name : MR.
AGE/SEX : 51Y/M
DOS : 02/04/2026
UHID NO : 109983
Physician Incharge: DM, Cardiologist
Procedure done : Coronary Angiography
Assisted by :
Approach by : Rt. Femoral
Catheters used : JL/IR-3.5-6F
Dye used : Visipaque
LMCA : Small and normal.
LAD : Average size vessel mid 90 % stenosis with thrombus.
Distal LAD discrete 90% stenosis.
Minor vessel and normal.

OM1 : Minor vessel and normal.
RCA : Average size vessel and mid 50 % stenosis.
IMPRESSION : Double Vessel Disease
RECOMMENDATIONS: PTCA/s to LAD (2 Stent)

records.summary: This is a typed coronary angiography report detailing findings from a procedure performed on a 51-year-old male patient. The report notes significant stenosis in the left anterior descending (LAD) artery and moderate stenosis in the right coronary artery (RCA), leading to an impression of double vessel disease. Recommendations include percutaneous transluminal coronary angioplasty (PTCA) with stenting in the LAD artery.
key_observations: Procedure: Coronary Angiography via right femoral approach.
Findings: 90% stenosis in mid LAD with thrombus, 90% stenosis in distal LAD, 50% stenosis in RCA.
Recommendation: PTCA with 2 stents in the LAD artery.
